title: Claude Earnings Call Q&A Sentiment Scorer
summary: Build an earnings call transcript parser that uses Claude to score the tone and evasiveness of the Q&A section (not just prepared remarks) within 2 hours of a call ending. Chiang et al. (2025) used LLM vector embeddings from 192K earnings call transcripts to quantify semantic alignment between analyst questions and executive answers as a proxy for transparency. LSEG MarketPsych's practitioner data shows companies expressing high 'disapproval' in earnings calls underperform meaningfully the following month. The signal is highest-impact in the 1-5 day post-call window — perfectly suited to Decifer's short-term trading horizon.
expected_impact: Chiang et al. 2025 (192K transcripts) show earnings call Q&A tone predicts next-period returns; LSEG MarketPsych found top-5% 'disapproval' calls underperform significantly the following month — adds event-driven catalyst layer
